specific_mechanism: RGFP: Renormalization-Group Fixed-Point regularization. For each transformer block l define K effective couplings c_l ∈ R^K via fixed observable functionals (attention spectral radius, MLP output kurtosis, residual entropy, etc.). Across layers compute discrete β_l = c_{l+1}-c_l. Add a regularizer R = Σ_l (c_l - c*)^T Σ_RG^{-1} (c_l - c*) pulling trajectories toward a learnable fixed point c*.
llm_application: RGFP regularization: at fine-tuning time, compute K=8 effective couplings per block (spectral radius of attention map, residual stream entropy, MLP kurtosis, sparsity ratio, etc.). β-function estimate per block β_l = c_{l+1}-c_l. Add penalty Σ_l ‖c_l-c*‖²_Σ + ‖β_l‖² to the training loss with strength λ_RG. c* is a learnable per-task fixed-point vector. The intent: enforce that the cross-depth trajectory of effective couplings approaches an RG fixed point typical of well-converged trained transformers.
novelty_claim: Multiple papers (AAAI 2025; arXiv 2510.25553; arXiv 2510.06508) treat DNN layers as RG transformations theoretically. But concrete fixed-point regularization on per-layer effective couplings as an explicit training term is less common.
motivation_justification: RG flow IS the mathematical structure of multi-scale coarse-graining. The β-function is the precise algebraic operator. Mechanism transfer is direct, not metaphor.
sub_mechanisms: M_1: K-dim per-layer effective coupling vector c_l
M_4: Learnable per-task c* fixed point
content_words: renormalization group beta function fixed point
spectral radius attention MLP kurtosis residual entropy
